$schema_note: Fixed evidence list for the pipeline soundness audit. Resolved by tools/audit-resolve.js; injected by tools/audit-inject.js. Paths are repo-root-relative. Edit the `space` block to re-target a different run. NOTHING here is free-discovery — the list is fixed; the resolver only confirms each item exists on disk.
_a_law_note: A prosecutes reasoning, so it always gets the operator vocabulary file in addition to the scout-selected DR law.
a_run_notes: **Operator tradeoff — the missing VOC (prosecute, do not excuse).** The operator DELIBERATELY omitted VOC (voice-of-customer / review-mine) to ship fast — a known, chosen tradeoff, not an oversight. Do NOT charge the omission as a mere gap and stop. Immediately prosecute whether the tradeoff is DR-DEFENSIBLE: does the DR law permit building a belief chain on an ASSERTED (not VOC-grounded) transformation when speed is the priority? If so, under what conditions — and were those conditions met here? Resolve VIOLATION / SURVIVED / CANNOT ASSESS against quoted law, like any other decision.
_a_run_notes_note: RUN-SPECIFIC. audit-inject.js injects these into EVERY Reviewer A segment as an 'OPERATOR PROSECUTION NOTES (A only)' section, and per-segment segments.<seg>.run_notes are appended for that segment. NEVER injected for B. Edit per run; reviewer-a.md stays generic.
_b_law_note: B is the prompt-blind grounding reviewer and must NEVER receive rule-theory (how-to-reason). audit-inject.js filters any law file whose basename equals or starts-with a denylist entry when --reviewer=B. differentiator-framework matches differentiator-framework__2_.md by prefix; angle.md and definitions.md match exactly. Enforced even if a scout slips one in.

A-collection._note: NO REVIEWER READS RAW BRAND PAGE COPY. The corpus/<brand>/clean/*.md extracted text is INTENTIONALLY not injected — the space-map.json IS the collection-stage record, and A prosecutes the classifier's REASONING against the pre-research plan + the space-map's own classified contents. Re-deriving classifications from raw page copy is not the audit's job. The .docx product description + .xlsx BOM are external; the pre-research-plan product section is the in-context substitute. Any collection point that would require raw copy -> A returns CANNOT ASSESS by design.

run_notes: **Funnel segment — two charges to flag explicitly.** The ANGLE-VALIDATION-AUDIT.md in your evidence is the architect's OWN input (the per-brand angle/validation 'lost-signal' doc), so the funnel reasoning is accountable to it:

1. The doc states transparency-as-verbal-hook is unproven by anyone in paid, and that curiosity is the proven winner at scale. If the funnel LED with transparency-as-claim anyway, that is a decision contradicting its own input — charge it (RULE-SOUNDNESS).

2. The doc's own provenance caveat flags the Flipper run-lengths and the '1M sold' figure as un-re-verified. If the architect rested an angle decision on those figures, that is a DATA-SUFFICIENCY violation — charge it. (The caveat is in your copy on purpose: it is evidence about how solid the input was.)

evidence.note: The per-brand angle/validation 'lost-signal' recovery doc — run BEFORE the funnel and consumed by the funnel architect (a real pipeline input, alongside the deep-pass store + chosen NTP). A gets it WHOLE — table, verdicts, 'what got lost', provenance caveat. See reviewer-a.md RUN-SPECIFIC FUNNEL CHARGES.

B._note: B gets NO prompts. Four outputs in sequence; space-map is the floor (not strip-audited internally). strip=true items must be routed through the strip-for-b agent BEFORE injection — keep verdicts/scores, drop the because.

evidence.strip_note: Keep the per-brand angle/volume/validation TABLE + mechanical figures (ad counts, run-lengths, '0 paid ads', backer/funding numbers) — INCLUDING the Flipper run-lengths and '1M sold' figures (B grounds against them). DROP the agent's verdicts, the 'what got lost' HIGH/MEDIUM editorializing, the 'safest bet from this corpus' recommendation, and the provenance-caveat PROSE. Reason: B grounds the funnel against facts, not against another agent's opinion.
